scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE District Court / Civil Court
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT WITH COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. This Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 CPC, as the agreement was entered into at {{PLACE_OF_CONTRACT}}, was to be performed at {{PLACE_OF_PERFORMANCE}}, and the breach occurred at {{PLACE_OF_BREACH}}; in any event, the Defendant resides or carries on business within the local limits of this Court at Bengaluru.

2. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court.

LIMITATION

3. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from date fixed for performance, or if no date fixed, when plaintiff has notice that performance is refused. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

4. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff entered into a written Agreement to Sell with the Defendant regarding the residential flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, for a total consideration of Rs. 85,00,000/-. The terms of the agreement are recorded in the document marked as Annexure A.

5. Pursuant to the said Agreement, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- to the Defendant on 10.01.2024 via RTGS (UTR: HDFC20240110987654). The proof of payment is contained in the bank receipt marked as Annexure B.

6. The Agreement stipulated that the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was to be paid by the Plaintiff on or before 10.07.2024 against the execution and registration of the sale deed. The Plaintiff arranged the necessary funds to discharge this obligation within the stipulated time.

7. The Plaintiff has duly performed all obligations required on her part under the Agreement and remained ready to pay the balance consideration and complete the transaction in accordance with the terms agreed upon between the parties.

BREACH PARTICULARS

8. On 05.06.2024, the Defendant sent a letter to the Plaintiff refusing to execute the sale deed, stating that he had received a higher offer for the property. A copy of the Defendant's refusal letter is marked as Annexure D.

9. On 20.06.2024, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice calling upon the Defendant to execute the sale deed and perform his part of the Agreement. The legal notice along with the postal receipt is marked as Annexure C.

10. The Defendant failed to comply with the legal notice and did not execute the sale deed despite the Plaintiff's readiness to perform the remaining obligations under the Agreement.

DAMAGES

11. Due to the Defendant's breach of the Agreement, the Plaintiff has suffered financial loss and is entitled to compensation for the direct consequences of the non-performance. In the alternative, the Plaintiff claims damages for the loss suffered due to the Defendant's failure to execute the sale deed as agreed.

LEGAL BASIS

12. The Plaintiff is entitled to a decree of specific performance of the agreement under Sections 10 and 14 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, the subject matter being such that the grant of compensation in money would not afford adequate relief.

13. The Plaintiff has been ready and willing to perform his/her part of the agreement at all material times within the meaning of Section 16(c) of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

CAUSE OF ACTION

14. The cause of action first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant committed default under the agreement and thereby caused the Plaintiff's right to sue for specific performance to accrue.

15. The breach was a single event. The Plaintiff's claim for specific performance subsists and remains enforceable in law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

16. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee on the plaint has been computed at Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka, with reference to {{COURT_FEE_COMPUTATION_BASIS}} and the applicable rate schedule. The plaint is tendered with the requisite court fee.

INTEREST

17. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of institution of the suit until the date of decree, under Section 34 CPC;
(b) Future interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of decree until realization of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree of specific performance directing the Defendant to perform and complete the agreement dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} in favour of the Plaintiff;
(b) Direct the Defendant to execute and register the necessary conveyance / sale deed in favour of the Plaintiff upon receipt of the balance consideration;
(c) In the alternative, award compensation / damages in lieu of specific performance;
(d) Award costs of the suit;
(e) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original agreement dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt (UTR: HDFC20240110987654)

Annexure C — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

Annexure D — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 17 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

PLAINTIFF

                                    THROUGH:
                                    {{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
                                    Advocate for the Plaintiff
                                    Enrollment No. {{ENROLLMENT_NO}}
                                    {{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
                                    Mobile: {{ADVOCATE_PHONE}}
                                    Email: {{ADVOCATE_EMAIL}}

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE Commercial Court (COMMERCIAL DIVISION)
AT Bengaluru

Commercial Suit No. __________ / 20____

TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd,
A company registered under the Companies Act,
Having its registered office at: MG Road, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Director, Ms. Priya Menon.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

CloudBridge Systems LLP,
A limited liability partnership,
Having its office at: Whitefield, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Designated Partner, Mr. Vikram Singh.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT UNDER SECTION 73 OF THE INDIAN CONTRACT ACT, 1872

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

COMMERCIAL COURT MAINTAINABILITY

1. The subject matter of the present suit arises out of a commercial dispute within the meaning of Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. The present suit is valued above the specified value of {{AMOUNT}} as prescribed under Section 12 of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, and is therefore maintainable before this Hon'ble Commercial Court.

JURISDICTION

2. This Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 CPC, as the agreement was entered into at {{PLACE_OF_CONTRACT}}, was to be performed at {{PLACE_OF_PERFORMANCE}}, and the breach occurred at {{PLACE_OF_BREACH}}; in any event, the Defendant resides or carries on business within the local limits of this Court at Bengaluru.

3. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court. This Hon'ble Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain commercial disputes under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

LIMITATION

4. The plaint is filed within limitation under Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, the period prescribed being three years from the date on which the contract was broken. The suit is filed well within the prescribed period.

SECTION 12A COMPLIANCE

5. {{SECTION_12A_COMPLIANCE_STATEMENT}}

FACTS OF THE CASE

6. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff entered into a written Software Development Agreement with the Defendant for the development of an ERP system.
7. Under the said Agreement, marked as Annexure P-1, the contract value was fixed at Rs. 45,00,000/- and the Defendant was obligated to deliver the first module by 01.05.2024.
8. The Plaintiff duly performed all obligations required on its part under the agreement by paying the first milestone payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- on 01.02.2024 via NEFT, evidenced by the receipt marked as Annexure P-3.

BREACH PARTICULARS

9. The Defendant failed to deliver the first module by the agreed deadline of 01.05.2024.
10. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant abandoned the project without completing the development.
11. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, marked as Annexure P-2, calling upon the Defendant to rectify the default, but the Defendant did not comply with the notice.

DAMAGES

12. The Defendant's failure to deliver the module and abandonment of the project caused direct financial loss to the Plaintiff.
13. The Plaintiff claims total damages of Rs. 32,00,000/-, comprising the refund of advance payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- and consequential damages of Rs. 12,00,000/-, which are the direct and natural consequence of the breach.
14. The consequential damages are quantified based on the loss assessment report by the auditor, marked as Annexure P-4.

LEGAL BASIS

15. The Defendant has committed a repudiatory breach by refusing to perform its obligations, entitling the Plaintiff to treat the contract as at an end and seek appropriate relief.

16. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which provides that when a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation for any loss or damage caused thereby, which naturally arose in the usual course of things from such breach, or which the parties knew, when they made the contract, to be likely to result from the breach of it.

CAUSE OF ACTION

17. The cause of action first arose on 15.06.2024 when the Defendant committed default under the agreement and thereby caused the Plaintiff's right to sue for damages to accrue.

18. The breach was a single event. The Plaintiff's claim for damages subsists and remains enforceable in law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

19. The present suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee on the plaint has been computed at Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka, with reference to {{COURT_FEE_COMPUTATION_BASIS}} and the applicable rate schedule. The plaint is tendered with the requisite court fee.

INTEREST

20. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of institution of the suit until the date of decree, under Section 34 CPC;
(b) Future interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of decree until realization of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) towards damages for breach of contract as particularised in the Schedule of Damages annexed hereto;
(b) Award pendente lite and future interest at such rate as the Court deems just under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
(c) Award costs of the suit including costs of the legal notice;
(d) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

SCHEDULE OF DAMAGES / PARTICULARS OF DAMAGES

The Plaintiff claims the following heads of damages with computation methodology:

(i) Actual and direct financial loss
     Amount claimed: Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/-
     Basis / methodology: {{ACTUAL_LOSS_BASIS}}
     Documentary proof: {{ACTUAL_LOSS_PROOF}}

TOTAL DAMAGES CLAIMED: Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/-

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure P-1 — Software Development Agreement dated 01.02.2024

Annexure P-2 — Legal notice dated 01.07.2024

Annexure P-3 — NEFT receipt (UTR: ICIC20240201456789)

Annexure P-4 — Loss assessment report by auditor

Annexure P-5 — Email correspondence showing abandonment

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 20 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

PLAINTIFF

STATEMENT OF TRUTH

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff / authorised representative of the Plaintiff herein, do hereby state on solemn affirmation that the contents of the above plaint and the facts stated in paragraphs 1 to 20 are true to my knowledge and belief and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

I understand that I shall be liable to proceedings under law for making a false statement.

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

                                    {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

                                    PLAINTIFF

                                    THROUGH:
                                    {{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
                                    Advocate for the Plaintiff
